avalancheActivity.comment:
Up to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Tuesday above approximately 1600 m.
In particular in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation and on very steep slopes small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized dry and wet avalanches are possible. They can in isolated cases be released, even by a single winter sport participant.

Restraint should be exercised because avalanches can sweep people along and give rise to falls.
avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow a moderate danger of dry and wet avalanches will prevail.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 20 cm of snow, and up to 30 cm in some localities, fell on Tuesday above approximately 1600 m.
The new snow is lying on a crust and generally at low and intermediate altitudes. The new snow is lying on surface hoar in some places on shady slopes at high altitude.

High Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the snowpack especially on shady slopes.

Over a wide area there is still a very large amount of snow.

avalancheActivity.comment:
In some regions up to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Tuesday above approximately 1800 m. The new snow is lying on top of a favourable old snowpack.

Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads. This applies especially on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m.

There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
avalancheActivity.highlights: The backcountry touring conditions are mostly favourable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
5 to 10 cm of snow fell on Tuesday above approximately 1600 m.
The old snowpack is well consolidated and its surface consists of loosely bonded snow lying on a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. The new snow is lying on surface hoar in some places on shady slopes at high altitude.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Weak layers exist in the snowpack especially on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
